title: bursera angustata
intro: Bursera angustata, also known as the Chachagüe, is a plant species belonging to the Burseraceae family, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Bursera angustata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which are made by boiling the leaves and bark in water or mixing them with other herbs.
